title: Spelling and Word Study
unitGoal: Students will practise Grade 5 spelling words through three built-in typed spelling assignments, then complete a final 30-word spelling test.

lessons.title: Tricky Vowel Patterns
lessons.learningGoal: Students will practise ten Grade 5 spelling words connected to tricky vowel patterns.
successCriteria: I can study each word carefully before I spell it.
I can use visible letters and sentence clues to help me spell.
I can spell the words independently after practice.
lessons.teacherOverview: Language Spelling Assignment 1 gives students ten built-in Grade 5 spelling words. Students copy each word, practise it with hidden letters, and then spell it from a sentence clue.
lessonContent: Practise words with vowel patterns that do not always sound the way they look.
The activity uses typed spelling instead of multiple choice so students show that they can spell the words themselves.
practiceIdeas: Share this assignment before the final 30-word spelling test.
Students can repeat the mission if they need more practice.
Review missed words before sharing the final spelling test.

studentActivityPlan.title: Language Spelling Assignment 1
studentActivityPlan.description: Practise words with vowel patterns that do not always sound the way they look.
studentActivityPlan.reward: Practice stars for correct spelling answers

quizPlan.title: Tricky Vowel Patterns Check
quizPlan.format: Typed spelling practice built into the mission. The final unit test checks all 30 words.

lessons.assessmentPlan: Use the mission completion and missed-word report to decide whether the student is ready for the final spelling test.

studentActivity.title: Language Spelling Assignment 1
studentActivity.mission: Practise words with vowel patterns that do not always sound the way they look.

steps.title: Tricky Vowel Patterns
steps.prompt: Before You Begin
steps.text: Some English words have vowel teams or sounds that are easy to mix up. Look carefully at the letter order before you type.

steps.title: Study Word 1
steps.prompt: Type this word: receive
steps.sentence: I will receive a letter in the mail.
steps.feedback: Remember: receive has e before i after c.

steps.title: Study Word 2
steps.prompt: Type this word: believe
steps.sentence: I believe the story is true.
steps.feedback: Believe uses ie in the middle.

steps.title: Study Word 3
steps.prompt: Type this word: neighbor
steps.sentence: Our neighbor helped carry the boxes.
steps.feedback: Neighbor uses eigh for the long a sound.

steps.title: Study Word 4
steps.prompt: Type this word: weight
steps.sentence: The weight of the backpack felt heavy.
steps.feedback: Weight also uses eigh for the long a sound.

steps.title: Study Word 5
steps.prompt: Type this word: brought
steps.sentence: She brought her notebook to class.
steps.feedback: Brought has ought at the end.

steps.title: Study Word 6
steps.prompt: Type this word: thought
steps.sentence: I thought carefully before I answered.
steps.feedback: Thought has ought at the end.

steps.title: Study Word 7
steps.prompt: Type this word: enough
steps.sentence: We have enough chairs for everyone.
steps.feedback: Enough ends with ough.

steps.title: Study Word 8
steps.prompt: Type this word: although
steps.sentence: Although it was raining, the game continued.
steps.feedback: Although also ends with ough, but it sounds different from enough.

steps.title: Study Word 9
steps.prompt: Type this word: journey
steps.sentence: The family began a long journey.
steps.feedback: Journey has our in the middle.

steps.title: Study Word 10
steps.prompt: Type this word: favorite
steps.sentence: My favorite book is on the shelf.
steps.feedback: Favorite has orite at the end.

steps.feedback: Remember: receive has e before i after c.

steps.feedback: Weight also uses eigh for the long a sound.

steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.

steps.feedback: Brought has ought at the end.

steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: I _____ carefully before I answered.
steps.feedback: Thought has ought at the end.

steps.feedback: Enough ends with ough.

steps.feedback: Although also ends with ough, but it sounds different from enough.

steps.feedback: Journey has our in the middle.

steps.sentence: My _____ book is on the shelf.
steps.feedback: Favorite has orite at the end.

lessons.teacherSummary: Language Spelling Assignment 1 is a student-shareable typed spelling assignment with 10 words.

lessons.title: Prefixes, Suffixes, and Word Parts
lessons.learningGoal: Students will practise ten Grade 5 spelling words connected to prefixes, suffixes, and word parts.
successCriteria: I can study each word carefully before I spell it.
I can use visible letters and sentence clues to help me spell.
I can spell the words independently after practice.
lessons.teacherOverview: Language Spelling Assignment 2 gives students ten built-in Grade 5 spelling words. Students copy each word, practise it with hidden letters, and then spell it from a sentence clue.
lessonContent: Practise words that grow from smaller word parts.
The activity uses typed spelling instead of multiple choice so students show that they can spell the words themselves.
practiceIdeas: Share this assignment before the final 30-word spelling test.
Students can repeat the mission if they need more practice.
Review missed words before sharing the final spelling test.

studentActivityPlan.title: Language Spelling Assignment 2
studentActivityPlan.description: Practise words that grow from smaller word parts.
studentActivityPlan.reward: Practice stars for correct spelling answers

quizPlan.title: Prefixes, Suffixes, and Word Parts Check
quizPlan.format: Typed spelling practice built into the mission. The final unit test checks all 30 words.

lessons.assessmentPlan: Use the mission completion and missed-word report to decide whether the student is ready for the final spelling test.

studentActivity.title: Language Spelling Assignment 2
studentActivity.mission: Practise words that grow from smaller word parts.

steps.title: Prefixes, Suffixes, and Word Parts
steps.prompt: Before You Begin
steps.text: Prefixes and suffixes can change a word. Spell the base word carefully, then add the word part in the right place.

steps.title: Study Word 1
steps.prompt: Type this word: disagree
steps.sentence: Friends can disagree and still speak kindly.
steps.feedback: Disagree begins with the prefix dis-.

steps.title: Study Word 2
steps.prompt: Type this word: preview
steps.sentence: We will preview the chapter before reading.
steps.feedback: Preview begins with pre-.

steps.title: Study Word 3
steps.prompt: Type this word: rewrite
steps.sentence: I need to rewrite the paragraph neatly.
steps.feedback: Rewrite begins with re-.

steps.title: Study Word 4
steps.prompt: Type this word: careless
steps.sentence: A careless mistake can change the answer.
steps.feedback: Careless ends with -less.

steps.title: Study Word 5
steps.prompt: Type this word: hopeful
steps.sentence: The team felt hopeful after practice.
steps.feedback: Hopeful ends with -ful.

steps.title: Study Word 6
steps.prompt: Type this word: movement
steps.sentence: The movement of the clouds was slow.
steps.feedback: Movement ends with -ment.

steps.title: Study Word 7
steps.prompt: Type this word: payment
steps.sentence: The payment was due on Friday.
steps.feedback: Payment ends with -ment.

steps.title: Study Word 8
steps.prompt: Type this word: teacher
steps.sentence: The teacher explained the directions clearly.
steps.feedback: Teacher ends with -er.

steps.title: Study Word 9
steps.prompt: Type this word: beautiful
steps.sentence: The garden looked beautiful in spring.
steps.feedback: Beautiful keeps beauty's beau at the beginning.

steps.title: Study Word 10
steps.prompt: Type this word: happiness
steps.sentence: True happiness is not the same as getting everything you want.
steps.feedback: Happiness changes y to i before -ness.

steps.feedback: Disagree begins with the prefix dis-.

steps.feedback: Careless ends with -less.

steps.feedback: Hopeful ends with -ful.

lessons.teacherSummary: Language Spelling Assignment 2 is a student-shareable typed spelling assignment with 10 words.

lessons.title: Academic Reading and Writing Words
lessons.learningGoal: Students will practise ten Grade 5 spelling words connected to academic reading and writing words.
successCriteria: I can study each word carefully before I spell it.
I can use visible letters and sentence clues to help me spell.
I can spell the words independently after practice.
lessons.teacherOverview: Language Spelling Assignment 3 gives students ten built-in Grade 5 spelling words. Students copy each word, practise it with hidden letters, and then spell it from a sentence clue.
lessonContent: Practise words students often use in reading, writing, and explaining answers.
The activity uses typed spelling instead of multiple choice so students show that they can spell the words themselves.
practiceIdeas: Share this assignment before the final 30-word spelling test.
Students can repeat the mission if they need more practice.
Review missed words before sharing the final spelling test.

studentActivityPlan.title: Language Spelling Assignment 3
studentActivityPlan.description: Practise words students often use in reading, writing, and explaining answers.
studentActivityPlan.reward: Practice stars for correct spelling answers

quizPlan.title: Academic Reading and Writing Words Check
quizPlan.format: Typed spelling practice built into the mission. The final unit test checks all 30 words.

lessons.assessmentPlan: Use the mission completion and missed-word report to decide whether the student is ready for the final spelling test.

studentActivity.title: Language Spelling Assignment 3
studentActivity.mission: Practise words students often use in reading, writing, and explaining answers.

steps.title: Academic Reading and Writing Words
steps.prompt: Before You Begin
steps.text: These words appear often in school work. Spell them carefully so your written answers are clear.

steps.title: Study Word 1
steps.prompt: Type this word: describe
steps.sentence: Please describe the setting of the story.
steps.feedback: Describe begins with de- and ends with -scribe.

steps.title: Study Word 2
steps.prompt: Type this word: explain
steps.sentence: Explain your answer using evidence.
steps.feedback: Explain has ai near the end.

steps.title: Study Word 3
steps.prompt: Type this word: compare
steps.sentence: Compare the two characters in the passage.
steps.feedback: Compare ends with -are.

steps.title: Study Word 4
steps.prompt: Type this word: evidence
steps.sentence: Use evidence from the text to support your idea.
steps.feedback: Evidence has three e sounds, but only one i.

steps.title: Study Word 5
steps.prompt: Type this word: reason
steps.sentence: Give one reason for your answer.
steps.feedback: Reason has ea in the middle.

steps.title: Study Word 6
steps.prompt: Type this word: solution
steps.sentence: The solution to the problem took three steps.
steps.feedback: Solution ends with -tion.

steps.title: Study Word 7
steps.prompt: Type this word: question
steps.sentence: Read the question before choosing an answer.
steps.feedback: Question also ends with -tion.

steps.title: Study Word 8
steps.prompt: Type this word: complete
steps.sentence: Complete the assignment before lunch.
steps.feedback: Complete has ete at the end.

steps.title: Study Word 9
steps.prompt: Type this word: important
steps.sentence: It is important to check your work.
steps.feedback: Important has ant at the end.

steps.title: Study Word 10
steps.prompt: Type this word: possible
steps.sentence: It is possible to improve with practice.
steps.feedback: Possible has double s and ends with -ible.

steps.title: Word 1
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: Please _____ the setting of the story.
steps.feedback: Describe begins with de- and ends with -scribe.

steps.title: Word 2
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: _____ your answer using evidence.
steps.feedback: Explain has ai near the end.

steps.title: Word 3
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: _____ the two characters in the passage.
steps.feedback: Compare ends with -are.

steps.title: Word 4
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.

steps.feedback: Evidence has three e sounds, but only one i.

steps.title: Word 5
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: Give one _____ for your answer.
steps.feedback: Reason has ea in the middle.

steps.title: Word 6
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: The _____ to the problem took three steps.
steps.feedback: Solution ends with -tion.

steps.feedback: Question also ends with -tion.

steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: _____ the assignment before lunch.
steps.feedback: Complete has ete at the end.

steps.title: Word 9
steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: It is _____ to check your work.
steps.feedback: Important has ant at the end.

steps.prompt: Use the visible letters and the sentence clue to spell the word.
steps.sentence: It is _____ to improve with practice.
steps.feedback: Possible has double s and ends with -ible.

steps.feedback: Describe begins with de- and ends with -scribe.

lessons.title: 30-Word Spelling Test
lessons.learningGoal: Students will spell all thirty words from the Grade 5 spelling unit independently.
lessons.teacherOverview: Final typed spelling test covering all three built-in spelling assignments.
lessons.teacherSummary: Share this after students have practised all three built-in spelling assignments.

unitAssessmentPlan.spellingAssignments: Three typed spelling assignments with ten words each.
unitAssessmentPlan.unitTest: One final typed spelling test covering all thirty words.
